Florida's statute of limitations gives most victims 4 years to file a claim — but that window is deceptive. Black box data, driver logs, and physical evidence can disappear within days. Trucking companies have rapid-response legal teams on the scene almost immediately. Victims deserve the same urgency.

reddit_title: Semi truck accident in Tallahassee, FL — what steps should you take to protect your case?
reddit_body:
If you've been in a semi truck accident in Tallahassee, the first thing to understand is that commercial truck cases are significantly more complex than standard car accidents. Trucking companies are required to maintain black box data, driver logs, maintenance records, and cargo manifests — all of which can be critical evidence. The problem is that this data can be deleted or overwritten quickly, so sending a legal preservation letter (spoliation notice) as early as possible is essential.

Florida generally gives truck accident victims 4 years from the date of the accident to file a civil claim, but don't let that timeline create a false sense of security. Insurance adjusters for large carriers move fast, and anything you say early in the process can be used to reduce your settlement. Avoid giving recorded statements without an attorney present, and document everything — photos, medical visits, witness contact info, and any communication from insurers.

Finding an attorney who specifically handles commercial truck litigation in Florida makes a real difference. If you're not sure where to start, wreckmatch.com is a free service that matches accident victims with experienced local attorneys — no pressure, just a faster way to find qualified help when it matters most.
